metadata.description: 借用检查器: Compile-time Analysis of References: A mutable reference or multiple immutable references cannot coexist simultaneously. To illustrate this with a simple Python code example, consider the following scenario where attempting to have both a mutable and an immutable reference to the same variable at the same time would result in a runtime error: ```python def test_references(): x = 10 # An immutable integer def try_to_mutate(): # This will raise a TypeError because you cannot modify an immutable object x += 1 def use_reference(): # This is fine as it just uses the reference without modifying it print(x) # Trying to mutate 'x' directly would fail, but this example shows the concept try_to_mutate() use_reference() # test_references() # Uncommenting this line will raise a TypeError if supported by your Python version ``` In this example, `x` is an immutable integer. Attempting to modify it within the `try_to_mutate()` function would result in a runtime error (`TypeError`). The `use_reference()` function simply prints the value of `x`, demonstrating the use of an immutable reference without modification.

data_pointers.title: 借用检查器
data_pointers.content_preview: 借用检查器: Compile-time Analysis of References: A mutable reference or multiple immutable references cannot coexist simultaneously. To illustrate this with a simple Python code example, consider the following scenario where attempting to have both a mutable and an immutable reference to the same variable at the same time would result in a runtime error: ```python def test_references(): x = 10 # An immutable integer def try_to_mutate(): # This will raise a TypeError because you cannot modify an immutable object x += 1 def use_reference(): # This is fine as it just uses the reference without modifying it print(x) # Trying to mutate 'x' directly would fail, but this example shows the concept try_to_mutate() use_reference() # test_references() # Uncommenting this line will raise a TypeError if supported by your Python version ``` In this example, `x` is an immutable integer. Attempting to modify it within the `try_to_mutate()` function would result in a runtime error (`TypeError`). The `use_reference()` function simply prints the value of `x`, demonstrating the use of an immutable reference without modification.

common_pitfalls: 在不可变引用作用域内尝试创建可变引用：即使不可变引用不再使用，只要其作用域未结束，编译器仍会阻止可变引用。应使用花括号缩小作用域或确保不可变引用在可变引用创建前被丢弃。
返回局部变量的引用：函数内创建的局部变量在函数结束后被销毁，返回其引用会导致悬垂指针。应返回参数引用或使用所有权转移。
在循环中同时持有可变引用和不可变引用：例如在迭代器上调用 `iter()` 后尝试修改集合，会导致编译错误。应使用 `iter_mut()` 或重新设计循环逻辑。
